_readme: This file encodes personal conventions for interpreting FitNotes training data. Full form hierarchies are documented here for future use when the comment-reading tool is built (Stage 4/5). Only unit/bar/set rules are injected into LLM prompts today.
general_principle: Form is assumed correct unless the comment explicitly states it was wrong. Do not infer form errors from ROM or completion terms.

unit_overrides.description: These exercises are logged in kg, not lbs. All other exercises use lbs (FitNotes stores as kg via conversion).

exercise_notes.Deadlift: Logged in kg from 2025-12-26 onwards. Before that (including 2025-12-20) was in lbs.
exercise_notes.Machine Wrist Extension: All 0.0kg entries = treat as 5kg (machine base resistance with no added plate).
exercise_notes.Hand Gripper: Usually kg. If comment for that set contains the word 'Pounds', that set was logged in lbs instead.

bar_weights_not_included.description: For all barbell and Smith machine exercises, logged metric_weight = plates only. Bar weight is never included in the logged value. Add bar weight to get true total.

names: first sessions before bar was named

Deadlift.note: Standard Olympic barbell. Consistent throughout. Logged weight = plates only in kg from 2025-12-26.

smith_machine.note: All Smith machine logged weights are in lbs (stored as kg by FitNotes conversion). Logged value = plates added only. Bar weight (20kg) not included.

counterbalance_supports.description: The gym Smith machine has counterbalance supports that reduce effective bar weight. Each support = 10kg counterbalance. Noted in comments.
counterbalance_supports.zero_logged_kg: 0kg logged = bodyweight only, no bar used

drop_set_notation.description: Sets sharing the same set number in comments (e.g. '2nd set', '3rd set') on the same exercise and date were performed back-to-back as a drop set with no rest between them.
examples: Two consecutive logged rows both commented '2nd set' = one drop set done back-to-back
Three rows all commented '3rd set' = one triple drop set, done consecutively

additional_markers.Back to back: Early notation (July 2024) for the same concept — consecutive sets no rest
additional_markers.1st set: Marks the first set in a drop sequence

warmup_sets.Flat Dumbbell Bench Press: Sets with comment 'Done first as a warm up' or 'Done first as a warmup' are warmup sets. Exclude from working set counts and strength analysis.
warmup_sets.dumbbell skull crusher: Sets at 3.4kg (7.5lbs) with comment 'Warmup for elbows' are warmup sets. Exclude from analysis.

hand_gripper_modes.Each hand / Single hand: Same thing — one hand at a time performing the exercise

ez_bar_curl_grips.Wide / Broad: Wider grip position on the EZ-bar
ez_bar_curl_grips.Narrow: Narrower grip position on the EZ-bar

form_quality_hierarchies._note: These hierarchies are documented for future use when the comment-reading tool is built. Form is assumed correct unless explicitly stated otherwise — terms like 'far', 'not touching', 'partial' describe incomplete ROM, not form errors.

Lat Pulldown.type: ROM hierarchy — bar pulled down to different depths
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Touching chest — full ROM, bar pulled to chest
Almost touching chest / Almost — bar very close to chest
Below the neck — bar pulled below neck level
Neck up — bar only pulled to neck height
Till chin / Till nose / Till forehead / Till eyebrow — progressively shorter range, describes exact stopping point
Partial — very short range
Half — minimal range
Touching chest
Almost / Almost touching
Below the neck
Neck up
Till chin / Till nose — shorter partial
Partial
Half

Seated Narrow V Shaped Row.type: ROM hierarchy — how close the attachment came to the body
Seated Narrow V Shaped Row.note: Close/Far describes attachment distance from body. No form error assumed.
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Close — full pull, attachment came all the way to the body
Almost — nearly full pull
Pretty far — didn't pull all the way, moderate ROM
Far — significant incomplete pull
Partial — short range
Half — very limited range

Wide Grip Cable Row.type: ROM hierarchy — how far the attachment was pulled
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Touching / Close — full pull to body
Almost / Almost touching — close but not complete
Not touching — didn't complete the pull
Partial — short range

Smith Machine Shrugs.type: Contraction quality at top — about the squeeze, not just ROM
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Squeezed properly at the top / Done properly — full contraction held
Almost fully squeezed — near complete contraction
Not squeezed properly / Did not squeeze completely — partial contraction
Halfs — shoulders not fully elevated
Partial — very short range

Barbell Upright Row.type: ROM hierarchy — how high the bar was pulled
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Till chest / Pushed till chest — full ROM, bar to chest level
Half / Stopped halfway — only went partway up
Partial — short range
form_breaks_noted_separately: Used momentum — momentum to complete rep
Used legs — leg drive to assist

Seated Machine Curl (Kg).type: ROM hierarchy — how fully the curl was completed
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Pushed to the top / Squeezed at the top — full contraction
Almost — close to full contraction
Half — curled halfway
Partial — very short range

Barbell Curl.type: Form quality tracking — no ROM hierarchy, tracks cheating
quality_best_to_worst: Controlled ups and downs — strict form
Swinging / Using back — significant form break
All back and shoulders — mostly not using biceps

terms.Half: Arms only lowered halfway before being pressed back up — incomplete eccentric

Incline Dumbbell Bench Press.type: Same as Flat — lowering depth and assistance

Decline Dumbbell Bench Press.type: Same as Flat — lowering depth and assistance

Lateral Dumbbell Raise.type: Two separate failure modes — not a single continuous hierarchy

failure_modes.Half: FORM issue — arms bent inward at elbow (not fully extended outward to the side). The arms went up but with bent elbows.
failure_modes.Partial / Not fully up: ROM issue — arms raised with correct form but not to full height
failure_modes.Close to body: ROM issue — arms not going out wide enough to the sides

Lateral Dumbbell Raise.note: Half and Partial describe different problems. Half = elbow bent. Partial = range too short.

terms.Fully up: Complete extension at top — full quad contraction
terms.Not fully up: Didn't achieve full extension at top
terms.Partial: Complete lowering but only partial lifting — goes all the way down but doesn't extend fully at top

Hamstring Curls Machine.type: ROM hierarchy — how fully the curl was completed
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Touching / Almost touching — heel reaching or nearly reaching glutes
Not touching — didn't fully curl
Half — halfway
Partial — short range
Disgracefully — very poor range and effort

Cable Triceps Extension.type: Contraction quality and ROM — not a simple hierarchy

terms.Squeezed at the bottom: Full contraction achieved at bottom = correct
terms.Not let it come fully up / Didn't go ful: Didn't allow full stretch at top on release
terms.Halfs / Almost till halfway: Only pressed halfway down on push

Reverse Cable Curls.type: Grip tracking and form quality — no ROM hierarchy

grip_types.Thumb up grip: Thumbs pointing up, neutral/overhand with thumbs up variant

terms.Moving elbow: Elbow swinging forward — explicit form error noted

Seated Machine Fly.type: Two separate failure modes tracked independently

rom_terms.Hands touching at the end: Full ROM — arms came fully together in front

form_errors_noted_explicitly.Bent elbows: Main form error — elbows bending too much
form_errors_noted_explicitly.Back lifted: Lifting off back pad — explicit form error

Behind The Back Wrist Curls.type: ROM hierarchy for wrist curl completion
hierarchy_best_to_worst: Fully up / Pulled upwards — wrist fully curled all the way up
Almost fully up — close to complete
Halfs / Half ups — wrist curled halfway
Partials — very short wrist curl
Barely up — fingers did curl but minimal wrist movement
Fingers only / X fingers only — grip maintained but wrist not curling, just holding
Held at the end — static hold after reps, no more movement possible
Complete rep — full wrist extension and return
Half — only halfway
Partial — very short range
Barely up — minimal movement
Held at the end — static hold at failure
Machine Wrist Extension.rest_pause: Break at rep X = rest-pause mid-set because grip gave out, readjusted, then continued the same set

Farmers Walk.type: Grip endurance tracking — not a form hierarchy
grip_states_best_to_worst: Normal grip till end — maintained full hand grip throughout
Pinkies start to slip — early sign of grip failing
Held by fingers at the end — grip partially failed
Couldn't hold at the end — complete grip failure

hierarchy_best_to_worst: Fully down — complete lowering, full stretch achieved
Almost fully down — close to full range
Half — only lowered halfway
Partial — very short range

technique_variants.Single hand / Last 2 single hand: One arm at a time — technique variant, not a form error

other_conventions.assisted_reps: Comments like 'Last X assisted' or 'All assisted' mean spotter helped with those reps. Count as real reps performed.
other_conventions.negatives: Comments with 'Negatives' = eccentric-only reps. Only the lowering phase performed.
other_conventions.disgracefully: User's personal term meaning very poor quality set — weight too heavy, significant compensation. Not a specific ROM term.
other_conventions.partial_general: When 'partial' appears without exercise-specific context, it means incomplete ROM on the concentric (lifting) phase.
